title: For Trump, a Promised Economic Boom Collides With the Costs of War
summary: President Trump said he had envisioned a growing economy and improving fortunes for American families in 2026. That appears at risk in his war with Iran.

title: FCC Chair Threatens to Revoke Broadcasters’ Licenses Over Iran War Coverage
summary: The comment from Brendan Carr came on the heels of a social media message from President Trump criticizing the news media’s coverage of the war with Iran.

title: Markwayne Mullin, Trump’s Homeland Security Pick, Got Wealthier Stock Trading in Congress
summary: Markwayne Mullin’s financial dealings take on new importance as the Senate considers his nomination to lead an agency whose budget has vastly expanded.

title: Trump Administration Turns to Migrant Workers to Help Farm Labor Shortage
summary: As the president’s immigration policies squeeze an already tight supply of farm labor, the Trump administration is making it cheaper to hire foreign farmworkers.

title: A Refugee Died After Border Agents Left Him at a Cafe. Fear Followed.
summary: Buffalo’s Arakan Rohingya community was rattled after a disabled man’s death. “Our worry comes from future incidents that may happen,” one resident said.

title: State Department Cuts Price of Renouncing U.S. Citizenship to $450
summary: The fee had been increased to $2,350 in 2014, prompting criticism and legal challenges from advocacy groups and Americans living abroad.

title: The Billionaire Backlash Against a Philanthropic Dream
summary: The Giving Pledge, once trendy among the world’s richest, has come upon hard times.

title: It’s Good to Be a Billionaire, Even at Tax Time
summary: Paying taxes would feel better if the truly rich were bearing a fair share, our columnist says.

title: In Texas, an Unyielding Gun Culture Jumps Off YouTube and Into Politics
summary: Brandon Herrera, a Republican candidate for Congress, built a large online fan base as a “guntuber.”

title: The $500 Million Mystery Will, Signed by Ghosts
summary: A seven-page document, mailed by an elusive figure, has set off a court battle over the estate of Tony Hsieh, the former chief executive of Zappos.

title: Why Minnesota Matters More Than Iran for America’s Future
summary: Moms donating their breast milk to strangers, dads taking someone else’s kids to school: Minnesotans showed a basic human impulse to look out for their neighbors.

title: Timothée Chalamet Was Supposed to Be a New Kind of Leading Man
summary: He’s no longer that heart-on-his-sleeve sensitive soft boy many people fell for.

title: And the Oscar Goes to …
summary: A guide to the 2026 Academy Awards.

title: To Save His Life, Our Restaurant Critic Reset His Appetite
summary: Pete Wells on the radical overhaul of his relationship with eating.

title: ‘Nobody Owns Us’: How Plans for a Google Data Center Divided an Oklahoma Town
summary: The volunteer fire department serving a rural area near Sand Springs declined a $250,000 donation from the tech giant. Now some residents are suing to block the proposed development.

title: Paula Doress-Worters, an Author of ‘Our Bodies, Ourselves,’ Dies at 87
summary: She wrote about postpartum depression when it was an unmentionable like abortion or birth control, and her research on her own suffering helped countless women.

title: Commuters, Rejoice! The New Portal Bridge in New Jersey Is Ready for Riders.
summary: The old Portal Bridge disrupted rail commutes in New Jersey for decades, frequently getting stuck when it swung to let boat traffic pass. Now a new bridge is open to the public.
